steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage points to forms that "straddle the line between prose and poetry," showing they lack a fixed, clear shape.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the clue
steps.content:
The blank describes literary forms that "straddle the line between prose and poetry" — forms with no fixed boundaries. Substitute each word into "there are more ______ forms":

A. Neutral means impartial or having no strong qualities. Forms straddling prose and poetry are not neutral — they are distinctively hybrid. This doesn't describe their defining feature.

B. Customary means usual or conventional. This is the opposite of what Davis describes; these forms are unconventional precisely because they don't follow established categories.

C. Amorphous means without definite shape or form. "More amorphous forms" — forms that borrow from fables and realist narratives, that straddle prose and poetry — are by definition shapeless and uncategorizable. ✓

D. Dispersed means scattered or spread out. This describes physical distribution or arrangement, not the nature of a literary form's structure — it doesn't apply here.

C is correct.
